So we'll put raw_value as that number, value as the same, unit "deg", coordinate_format "decimal_degrees". For proper motions: pmra, pmdec from Gaia, so same frame/epoch, units mas yr-1. The paper states they compute heliocentric distance from parallax: d = 1/parallax. So we could compute it, but the extraction skill says do not recompute values unless the paper gives the converted value. The paper doesn't list heliocentric distance in these tables. So we should not include distance in observed_phase_space if not available. However, the distance is a derived quantity; we can leave it absent. We have parallax. Also proper motion and radial velocity. So observed_phase_space will contain ra, dec, parallax, proper_motion_ra, proper_motion_dec, radial_velocity. Nothing for distance.
